meta.note: Manual fallback data. Sourced from FIFA official site, Wikipedia (CC BY-SA 4.0), and openfootball (CC0). Update this file if automated fetch fails.
dataQualityWarnings: Fallback data - live scores not available. Automated fetch may have failed.

historicalWorldCups.memorableFact: Diego Maradona's 'Hand of God' goal and 'Goal of the Century' vs England.
Lowest-scoring World Cup ever, nicknamed 'Italia 90' for its dramatic knockout stages.

historicalWorldCups.goldenBoot: Hristo Stoichkov (Bulgaria) / Oleg Salenko (Russia, 6 goals each)
historicalWorldCups.memorableFact: Roberto Baggio missed the decisive penalty. First WC decided on penalties in the final.
Zinedine Zidane scored two headers in the final. Ronaldo played despite a mysterious pre-match illness.
First WC held in Asia. Turkey finished 3rd; South Korea reached the semi-finals as the biggest Asian surprise.
Zidane was sent off for headbutting Materazzi in his final game. Italy's 5th title.
First World Cup on African soil. Spain's tiki-taka won its first World Cup. Vuvuzelas became iconic.

historicalWorldCups.finalVenue: Estádio do Maracanã, Rio de Janeiro
historicalWorldCups.memorableFact: Germany's 7–1 demolition of host Brazil in the semi-final ('Mineirazo') is the biggest WC semi-final result.
Croatia reached their first-ever final. France's youngest ever World Cup squad. VAR used for the first time.
Messi won his first World Cup. The final is widely regarded as the greatest ever. Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the final.

facts.title: First 48-Team World Cup
facts.body: The 2026 edition is the first to feature 48 teams — up from 32 — across 12 groups, making it the largest World Cup in history.

facts.title: Three-Nation Host
facts.body: For the first time ever, the World Cup is hosted by three nations: Canada, Mexico, and the USA — spanning two continents.

facts.title: 17 Spectacular Stadiums
facts.body: The tournament spans 17 venues across 16 cities: 11 in the USA, 3 in Canada, and 3 in Mexico, with capacities ranging from 36,000 to over 87,000.

facts.title: 104 Matches Total
facts.body: 2026 features 104 matches: 72 in the group stage (12 groups × 6 matches), plus 32 knockout matches including a new Round of 32.

facts.title: Mexico's Third World Cup
facts.body: Mexico becomes the first country to host the World Cup three times (1970, 1986, 2026), with Estadio Azteca hosting matches in all three editions.

facts.title: USA Returns After 32 Years
facts.body: The USA last hosted the World Cup in 1994. After 32 years, they return — this time with 11 venues, the most of any host nation.

facts.title: Biggest Upset So Far
facts.body: Argentina, the defending champions, were held to a 2-2 draw by Japan — the biggest result based on FIFA ranking difference so far.

facts.title: Fastest Goal
facts.body: Kylian Mbappé's 5th-minute strike vs South Korea is the fastest goal of the tournament so far (Day 7).

facts.title: Santiago Giménez — Top Scorer (Mexico)
facts.body: Mexico's Santiago Giménez has scored 3 goals in 3 games to become the standout performer from Group A.

facts.title: Best Third-Place Teams Advance
facts.body: 8 of the 12 third-place teams qualify for the Round of 32. Teams are ranked by points, goal difference, goals scored, and fair play.

facts.title: Estadio Azteca — World Cup Legend
facts.body: Estadio Azteca has hosted the 1970 and 1986 World Cup finals. In 2026, it hosts the opening match — becoming the first stadium to host 3 World Cup openers.

facts.title: Defending Champions Argentina
facts.body: Argentina arrive as defending champions after their dramatic 2022 Qatar final victory over France on penalties, led by the legendary Lionel Messi.
